rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO** CONSERVATORSHIP OF NORMA SHIRAISHI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Transfer Of Property Belonging To The Conservatorship Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/10/2020. The following issues remain unaddressed: 1) A courtesy copy of the response (filed 03/18/2020) has not been received by the assigned examiner. 2) The proof of service (filed 06/22/2020) does not show service of process on Old Republic Title Company in compliance with CCP §§ 415.10 et seq. See Probate Code § 851(a)(2). 3) The notice and acknowledgment of receipt (filed 03/11/2020) does not state whether the attorney represents Syed Husain in both capacities (trustee and individual/real estate agent). 4) The response must be signed or verified by Syed Husain as a joining party. Probate Code § 1020 & CRC 7.103(a).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ODYSSEE WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. please review the 5th amended general order re the strong preference for remote appearances. as a result of the covid-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are highly encouraged to appear remotely. remote appearance can be by court call (information is in the 5th amended general order) or by videoconference using Zoom. to appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click join meeting, and use the ID and password below. Webinar ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 to appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the Webinar ID and password above. Issues: 1) The original petition had no accounting attached. The 12/18/17 Supplement has dates 04/26/16 to 07/31/17. The second supplement has dates 07/01/16 to 07/31/17. The first amended second account has dated 07/01/2016 to 07/31/2018. The accounting needs to need to be amended as a 2 year accounting to begin 07/20/16 as the prior accounting ended 07/19/2016. 2) Prior Examiner Notes Regarding Heirs. " On 1/18/17, this Court authorized Petitioner to pay $2,250 to HeirSearch.com to search for Decedent's paternal heirs. " On 7/14/17, Petitioner filed a Research Summary by Heirsearch.com [copy in file]. o This summary stated that due to challenges in this research it had only been able to identify Decedent's parents and that an additional 20 hours of research would be necessary to identify the paternal heirs. o The Petition states that Decedent's mother is Lola Williams. The Research Summary shows that Decedent's mother was Martha James [illegible surname]. o Petitioner was asked to explain the discrepancy. Petitioner attached a declaration reciting family ancestry and attaching various documents. However there are no official records showing that Lola was Decedent's mother. o iv. If the mother is not Lola Williams, how is James related to Decedent? " On 3/15/19, Petitioner filed another Research Summary [copy in file]. o It appears that Decedent had a half-brother named Joseph M. Williams, Jr., who died in 2012. The researcher was waiting for documentation to confirm this. o Upon confirmation, the researcher will determine whether Joseph, Jr. has any surviving issue. o Researcher will also follow-up re a possible adoption of Decedent by Lola Williams.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JAMES F. TAYLOR LIVING TRUST DATED 12/30/97 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/10/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) Given the reported value of $9,984.44 as of the account ending date, should the bond be maintained in the reduced amount of $10,000 per LRSF 14.44? 2) Paragraph 12 of the petition alleges that Exhibit B describes the time expended by the petitioner's attorney in representing the petitioner as conservator, but Exhibit B appears to describe services rendered to the petitioner in his trustee capacity. 3) Paragraph 14 of the petition does not contain descriptions required by CRC 7.703(e)(1)(A)& (C). 4) Paragraph 25 of the petition alleges that no notice is required to the Director of Health Services while referring to Probate Code § 1461 which concerns notice to the Directors of State Hospitals and Developmental Services. Did the petitioner's allegation concern notice required under § 215? 5) The summary of account must be on a mandatory Judicial Council form GC-400(SUM)/GC-405(SUM). CRC 5.575(a)(1). 6) The total amount of disbursements stated in the summary of account is different from the amount of total payments set forth on Schedule C-1. 7) What was the purpose of each bridge loan on 05/17/2018 & 07/13/2018 reported as capital receipts on Schedule A? 8) Each category of receipts must be subtotaled. CRC 7.575(c). 9) Why did the petitioner receive his trustee fees of $1,940.82 on 12/01/2017 from this trust (and not from the residue of the beneficiary's trust)? 10) Purchases of additional shares of NEFZX & OIBAX with reinvested dividends must be itemized on Schedule G. 11) No courtesy copy of the account statement (filed 06/10/2020) has been provided. It is unclear whether the petitioner's attorney has the original statement. The attorney's declaration alleges that the attorney has the original and/OR certified copies. Also, what did the attorney mean by "certified" copies? *The petitioner and his attorneys need to comply with CRC 1.201 to protect personal privacy. Only the last 4 digits of financial account numbers may be disclosed in pleadings (viewable to the public in PES & PTR cases unless sealed).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSEPH T. SHU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/03/2020. A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: 1) Paragraph 5 of the petition alleges that no notice is required under Probate Code § 9202(b) because no "heir" has been or is currently incarcerated, but such notice is also required if any "beneficiary" has been or is currently incarcerated. As the devisee is not the decedent's heir, an allegation is needed as to whether the devisee is a beneficiary within the meaning of the section. 2) Paragraph 14 of the petition alleges that all cash in the decedent's estate is being held in interest bearing accounts, but no cash is reported as an asset on hand. 3) Probate Code 10831(d) requires that the requested compensation and costs of the petitioner's attorney be paid "out of the estate". Has the petitioner in her beneficiary capacity advanced sufficient funds to the estate?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAYMOND C. YIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/03/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proposed orders have been provided. 2) No proof of publication is on file. 3) The decedent's aka name stated in the caption and item 2 of the notice of petition to administer estate is incomplete. 4) Item 10 of the notice of petition to administer estate states the petitioner's name, even though the attorney box was checked. 5) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) The self-proving box was not checked at item 3f(2) of the petition. B) Item 5a(3) or (4) of the petition must be completed. C) What is Carole Finnegan's relationship to the decedent? D) The dates of death must be specified for all deceased beneficiaries at item 8 of the petition. The personal representative of each post-deceased beneficiary must be identified. If no personal representative has been appointed, heirs or devisees of the post-deceased beneficiary must be identified. See LRSF 14.26(e)(2)&(3). Notice must be given to the persons identified. CRC 7.51(e)(1). E) The name, relationship, age, and address of the trustee of the devisee trust must be clearly identified at item 8 of the petition. See LRSF 14.26(D)(2). If the petitioner is the sole trustee, all beneficiaries of the trust under Probate Code § 1208(b) must be identified at item 8 of the petition. Notice must be given to the persons identified.
